items.title: Road maintenance needs joint responsibility from elected reps and agencies, says Ahmad Maslan after Johor inspections
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Road maintenance requires the commitment and cooperation of all stakeholders, including elected representatives and relevant agencies, to ensure issues are addressed promptly, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said. He said he has instructed the Public Works Department (JKR) to expedite repairs on damaged roads and urged all parties to play their respective roles in ensuring the maintenance process runs smoothly. Ahmad was commenting on the actions of Pakatan Harapan candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state seat, Dr Maszlee Malik, who recently drove along Jalan Tebrau to assess road conditions in the area.

items.title: Polish president vetoes student rights, maritime bills in setback for government
items.summary: WARSAW: Polish President Karol Nawrocki on Thursday vetoed two government-backed bills, rejecting legislation establishing school students' rights ombudsmen as "ideological" and blocking a maritime transport law that he said would implement elements of the European Union's Green Deal. Announcing the decision, Nawrocki said the bill creating student rights ombudsmen would increase bureaucracy and politicise Poland's education system rather than strengthen protections for pupils, Anadolu Ajansi reported. Instead of safeguarding students' rights, the legislation would create a "school prosecutor's office for pursuing anonymous denunciations", he wrote on the social media platform X, arguing it would lead to the ideological influence of schools.

items.title: #NSTviral: Viral video stirs debate over motorcycle parking habits [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A video showing motorcycles parked haphazardly in front of a restaurant has sparked debate online over the parking habits of motorcyclists in Malaysia. The post, uploaded by Threads user @jokerasya, showed several motorcycles parked in an area not designated for them, despite proper motorcycle parking bays appearing to be available nearby. the user wrote.

items.title: Trump Jr.'s gun retailer could gain from proposed home firearm delivery rule
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Donald Trump Jr. helped take an online retailer known as the "Amazon of guns" public last year. If finalised, the rule would be among the most consequential changes to the US gun policy in two decades, potentially driving huge growth in online gun sales, according to ten industry officials, store owners, and gun-control advocates interviewed by Reuters. Some gun shop owners, industry officials and gun control advocates argue the direct shipping of firearms poses significant public safety and security risks and threatens the viability of small brick-and-mortar gun shops.

items.title: Anthony Loke: Govt to seek audience with Selangor Sultan to explain LRT3 implementation issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — The Madani government will seek an audience with the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, to provide further clarification on the LRT3 Shah Alam Line, following the Ruler’s remarks on the project’s cost and delays on Wednesday. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the move was prompted by the ruler’s concerns over the project’s implementation. Also present were Pakatan Harapan (PH) Communications Director and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, PH’s Johor State Election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari and several PH candidates contesting in the Muar area.

items.title: Fed seen less likely to raise rates as job growth slows
items.summary: NEW YORK: Federal Reserve policymakers have less reason to raise interest rates later this month after a government report showed the US economy added far fewer jobs than expected over the past two months. Traders in short-term interest rate futures now see less than a 20 per cent chance of a rate hike in July, although they continue to view a September increase as the more likely outcome. Fed funds futures contracts imply about a 60 per cent chance of a September rate hike versus the policy rate remaining in the current range of 3.50 per cent to 3.75 per cent, down from about a 75 per cent probability before the jobs report.

items.title: Maszlee: Pakatan open to all partners for Johor state government
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 3 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is ready to align with any interested party to form a state government following the Johor state election, according to Johor PKR state leadership council vice-chief Maszlee Malik. The PH Puteri Wangsa candidate was countering a hardline stance taken by Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal, who recently declared that his coalition would refuse to work with PH. Dr Sahruddin, who also serves as the Johor Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) chief, stated that PN would not enter a coalition state government with PH due to a fundamental clash in beliefs.

items.title: Gold gains more than 2pct after weak US payrolls report
items.summary: Spot gold rose 2.2 per cent to US$4,116.54 per ounce as of 1.20pm EDT (1720 GMT). US gold futures settled 1.1 per cent higher at US$4,125.70. The US dollar index fell 0.5 per cent, making US dollar-denominated metals cheaper for holders of other currencies.

items.title: Cooling US jobs data buys the Fed and stock market more time
items.summary: NEW YORK: A weaker-than-expected June jobs report has provided relief for US equities, easing concerns that a resilient labour market could force the US Federal Reserve to adopt a more hawkish stance on interest rates and weigh on technology stocks that have driven this year's market rally. US job growth slowed more than expected in June, while payroll gains for the previous two months were revised lower, the Labour Department said on Thursday, pointing to a cooling but still stable labour market. The report could buy the stock market more time as investors grapple with soaring valuations, sharp swings in trillion-dollar technology stocks and periodic sell-offs that have fuelled concerns some parts of the market may be in bubble territory.

items.title: Ringgit opens firmer as weak US jobs data lifts rate cut hopes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened firmer against the US dollar on Friday after weaker-than-expected US jobs data for June reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) could adopt a more cautious approach to interest rates. This suggests the ringgit could appreciate further against the US dollar in the near term. "Yesterday, the ringgit closed 0.33 per cent higher against the US dollar at RM4.0805.

items.title: Russian attacks kill two, injure 8 in Ukraine overnight, officials say
items.summary: July 3 (Reuters) - Two people were killed and another eight injured in Russian overnight attacks on Ukraine, officials said on Telegram in the early hours of Friday. In the bordering Sumy region, two were killed and one injured after Russian drones hit a private house, said Oleh Hryhorov, head of the regional military administration. In the central Ukrainian city of Kryvyi Rih, hometown of President Volodymyr Zelenskiy, seven people were injured after a Russian missile strike on the densely built urban area, said Oleksandr Vilkul, the head of the city's defence council.

items.title: Do not open until 2276: US burying time capsule to mark July 4
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Americans living in 2276 will get a taste of what their ancestors today think best represents the nation thanks to a time capsule to be buried this July 4. It is not supposed to be opened for another 250 years, in 2276. Another time capsule scheduled for opening when the United States turns 500 years old was unveiled a few days ago in Washington.

items.title: MOSTI to prioritise talent development ahead of AMMSTI-23
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (MOSTI) will place talent development at the forefront of its agenda as Malaysia prepares to host the 23rd Asean Ministerial Meeting on Science, Technology and Innovation (AMMSTI-23) next year. "Besides talent development, our focus areas include energy transition, Artificial Intelligence (AI), digitalisation, advanced materials, nanotechnology, including hydrogen, and biotechnology," he said. In another development, Chang said MOSTI remains committed to supporting other ministries in strengthening Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes to better meet current industry demands, although TVET does not fall under the ministry's direct jurisdiction.

items.title: Dollar slides after soft jobs report, yen surges
items.summary: NEW YORK: The US dollar fell sharply on Thursday after the closely watched June employment report showed US employers added far fewer jobs than expected, while the Japanese yen surged as traders braced for possible intervention by Japanese authorities. The unemployment rate fell to 4.2 per cent from 4.3 per cent. The euro gained 0.52 per cent to US$1.1435 and reached US$1.1472, the highest since June 22.

items.title: Dow jumps to record closing high after soft US jobs data, Nasdaq down with chip shares
items.summary: The unemployment rate was 4.2 per cent, in line with expectations of 4.3 per cent. For the September meeting, hike expectations dimmed to 55 per cent from 64.1 per cent. Shares of Nvidia fell 1.4 per cent while SanDisk dropped 14.1 per cent.

items.title: Algerians vote for new parliament amid public apathy
items.summary: ALGIERS, (Algeria ): Algerians voted to elect a new parliament in a ballot yesterday marked by apathy and the disqualification of several hopefuls. About 25 million eligible voters are choosing 407 lawmakers in the lower house of parliament from 1,235 candidates amid cost-of-living concerns and shrinking freedoms, according to rights groups. Polling stations, which opened at 8:00 am local time (0700 GMT) and closed at 8:00 pm, were nearly empty in the capital Algiers as of early afternoon, an AFP journalist reported.

items.title: Resilient Bursa in June sets stage for second half upside [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia showed resilience through June's market volatility, supported by a strong domestic institutional investor base that helped cushion the market against external shocks. JP Morgan equity strategists Yen Voo and Khoi Vu said Malaysia's outlook remains supported despite a firmer US dollar and continued foreign selling pressure, with domestic liquidity and earnings resilience expected to anchor performance into the second half of 2026. Technology stocks experienced heightened volatility in June as a global technology sell-off and escalating geopolitical tensions rattled investor sentiment, but the sector remained among Bursa Malaysia's strongest performers.

items.title: Google loses fight against record €4.1 billion EU antitrust fine
items.summary: BRUSSELS: Alphabet's Google on Thursday lost its long-running fight against a record EU antitrust fine for using its Android mobile operating system to block rivals, a court ruling seen as likely to boost Europe's crackdown on Big Tech. Google lost an appeal against that in 2021. The Commission fined Google €4.34 billion for agreements that forced phone manufacturers to pre-install Google Search, the Chrome browser and the Google Play app store on their Android devices, while preventing them from using rival operating systems.

items.title: PublicInvest sees brent easing to US$70–US$85 as Iran risk premium fades
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Brent crude is expected to trade in a softer range of US$70 TO US$85 per barrel in the second half of 2026, as geopolitical tensions ease following a US–Iran interim agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) said the agreement, reached after 111 days of conflict, has triggered a sharp unwinding of the earlier war risk premium, with Brent retreating from an intraday high of US$126 per barrel to around US$72 as disrupted barrels gradually return to the global market. PublicInvest said it now expects Brent to average US$83 per barrel in 2026, revising its earlier assumptions to reflect a strong first half price spike followed by a lower trading range in the second half of 2026.

items.title: Tropicana taps hospitality for recurring income, partners Accor for Genting project
items.summary: The property company has partnered with Accor Group to launch Mercure Living Genting Highlands, billed as Malaysia's first Accor-branded condotel, at its Tropicana Grandhill development in Pahang. Tropicana said the development has achieved an 88 per cent take-up rate and is about 30 per cent complete. Accor said the launch of Mercure Living Genting Highlands supports its expansion of extended-stay accommodation in Malaysia.

items.title: AirAsia poised for comeback as lower oil prices support expansion plans, says Fernandes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia Group is preparing for a major expansion, with plans to restore lower airfares, add new routes and improve connectivity as easing oil prices and improving market conditions bolster the airline's recovery, Capital A Bhd chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes said. Fernandes cited AirAsia's longstanding relationship with GE Aerospace, recalling a recent visit to the company's headquarters in Cincinnati. He said GE Aerospace had backed AirAsia from its early days by supplying engines when the airline was still building its business, describing the relationship as one founded on trust and mutual commitment.

items.title: Malaysia's healthcare sector enters high-growth phase [WATCH]
items.summary: This will be driven by an ageing population, rising non-communicable diseases (NCDs), private sector expansion and accelerating digital health adoption, its head of healthcare and life sciences for Asia Pacific Rathanesh Ramasundram said. Ageing Population To Drive Healthcare Demand Rathanesh said Malaysia is on track to become an ageing nation, with around 15 per cent of the population aged 60 and above by 2030, increasing to 21 per cent by 2044. Private Healthcare, Medical Tourism Gain Momentum Malaysia's private healthcare sector, comprising around 290 private hospitals, is expanding rapidly amid rising insurance penetration and growing middle-class demand.

items.title: Giant century-old tree crashes onto homes, traps resident
items.summary: Paya Terubong Fire and Rescue Station operations supervisor Mohd Zaini Zainudin said firefighters received an emergency call at 8.48pm. He said when they arrived, they found a resident trapped inside one of the houses, not due to injuries but because the fallen tree had blocked the only exit. He said one house sustained minor damage to its gate and roof and was unoccupied, while the second house suffered damage to its gate, car porch roof and one vehicle.

items.title: Russia strikes Kyiv, killing 27, as both sides promise escalation
items.summary: KYIV: Ukraine and Russia on Thursday vowed fresh assaults in their over-four-year war after Moscow launched a massive barrage on Kyiv, killing at least 27 people, tearing open apartment buildings and sending tens of thousands to shelters. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his forces would "definitely" retaliate for the overnight pummelling as he inspected an apartment block that was partially destroyed. The European Union's top diplomat proposed new sanctions on Moscow, as Zelenskyy pressed the United States for licences to manufacture Patriot air-defence missiles.

items.title: Flowers and a raised fist: Teheran's Grand Mosalla readies for Khamenei farewell
items.summary: Teheran: At the entrance to the vast religious complex where Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's body is set to lie in state from Saturday, dozens were hard at work under an intense heatwave to prepare for the slain supreme leader's grand funeral. Security was on high alert ahead of the start of the funerary activities for Iran's ruler of three decades, with dozens of personnel stationed at the main entrance to the Grand Mosalla, methodically stopping each car in the vicinity. Within, the walls were draped in massive portraits of the late Khamenei, alongside black flags of mourning and red ones symbolising martyrdom and vengeance.

items.title: Mother claims son was killed for refusing to join secret society [WATCH]
items.summary: R. Arun Kumar was hacked to death on May 21. Seven suspects were charged with his murder last month. Breaking her silence for the first time since the murder, Arun's mother, K. Devi, said her son was brutally killed for not wanting to join a secret society gang.

items.title: Monthly adjustment system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Consumers are now seeing changes in their electricity bills in line with changing energy consumption patterns, including hotter weather and the growing number of people working from home. The changes are not arbitrary but are linked to a more transparent and equitable energy cost adjustment mechanism — the automatic fuel adjustment (AFA). HOW AFA WORKS The AFA is a monthly adjustment mechanism that factors in changes in global fuel prices, foreign exchange rates and actual generation costs to ensure the tariff system remains relevant to current energy market conditions.

items.title: New Malaysian passports: When and where they are rolling out at the 69 locations nationwide plus seven outside of the country
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 3 — If your Malaysian passport is expiring soon and you want to apply for the newly-launched passport version, here is a quick guide on how soon you can get it in the Immigration Department’s 69 offices nationwide and seven locations outside Malaysia. Here is the full list from the Immigration Department of the dates when you can apply for the new passports as they will be rolled out in seven stages locally: These are the 14 Immigration offices where you can already apply for the new Malaysian passport (available since July 1): • The Putrajaya headquarters, Menara Ikhlas; • Jalan Duta, Kuala Lumpur; • Kelana Jaya; • KLIA; • Melaka; • Seremban; • Eight UTC locations: UTC Puduraya, UTC Selangor, UTC Johor Bahru, UTC Pasir Gudang, UTC Ipoh, UTC Kedah, UTC Sungai Petani, UTC Seberang Prai. Next Monday (July 6) and onwards in eight locations: • Kajang, Setia Tropika, Kulai Jaya, Kulim; • UTC Sentul, UTC Keramat, UTC Melaka, UTC Pulau Pinang (Komtar) Next Thursday (July 9) and onwards in seven locations, including in Pahang, Kelantan and Terengganu: • Shaftsbury Putrajaya, Shah Alam (PKNS), Kluang, Batu Pahat; • UTC Kuantan, UTC Kota Bharu, UTC Kuala Terengganu July 13 and onwards in nine locations: • Kuala Selangor, Wangsa Maju, Puchong, Port Klang, Besut, Segamat, Muar, Kukup, Taiping July 21 and onwards in 10 locations, including in Perlis: • Lumut, Teluk Intan, Alor Setar, Kuala Langat, Kuala Kubu Baru, Temerloh, Raub, Port Dickson, Mersing, UTC Perlis By the end of this month, it will also start to be available in parts of Sabah and Sarawak: July 27 and onwards in 11 locations: • Chukai, Pengkalan Hulu, Cameron Highlands, Bintulu, Sri Aman, UTC Kuching, UTC Miri, UTC Sibu, UTC Kota Kinabalu, UTC Tawau, UTC Keningau August 3 and onwards in 10 locations: • Langkawi, Bukit Kayu Hitam, Kuching, Lawas, Limbang, Kota Kinabalu, Sandakan, LTA Kota Kinabalu, Lahad Datu, UTC Labuan If you are based abroad, this is when and where the new passports will be available at Immigration attache offices in Saudi Arabia, Indonesia, Hong Kong, Singapore, Australia, the UK and US: August 18 and onwards: Jeddah, Jakarta, Hong Kong August 25 and onwards: Singapore, Melbourne, London, New York The new Malaysian international passport.

items.title: UPNM developing rocket, missile tech modules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Pertahanan Nasional Malaysia (UPNM) will make it compulsory next year for engineering students to take modules on missile and rocket technology, as part of efforts to develop Malaysia's defence expertise. UPNM vice-chancellor Lieutenant-General Datuk Arman Rumaizi Ahmad said: "We want to produce experts among the new generation so that when this industry grows, it is led entirely by local youths, thus, safeguarding the sovereignty of the country's defence assets without having to depend on or be dominated by external parties." Arman said the initiative was backed by strategic partnerships with Australian institutions that have expertise in the field, including the University of Southern Queensland (UniSQ) and the University of New South Wales.

items.title: TNB to plant 25,000 mangroves
items.summary: MANJUNG: Tenaga Nasional Bhd on Tuesday launched a programme to plant 25,000 mangrove trees at the Tanjung Burung Mangrove Forest in Pantai Remis, Perak. TNB president and chief executive officer Datuk Shamsul Ahmad said the plan under the My Brighter Green Initiative (MBGI) aimed to restore mangrove ecosystems, boost coastal conservation awareness and foster community participation in sustainability. Shanmugaraj and TNB chief sustainability officer Leo Pui Yong.

items.title: Sony to stop releasing new PlayStation games on discs from 2028
items.summary: TOKYO: Sony said it will stop releasing new games for its PlayStation consoles on physical discs from 2028, as the company accelerates its shift towards digital distribution, German Press Agency (dpa) reported. From 2028, all new PlayStation titles will be available only in digital form, Sony said on Wednesday. Sony said games released before 2028, as well as existing titles, would continue to be available in their current formats.

items.title: Searching for Suu Kyi in capital of confusion
items.summary: NAYPYIDAW: Where is Aung San Suu Kyi? Myanmar's deposed leader is under house arrest in the capital here, according to her captors, but exactly where is hard to say in a city experts describe as purpose-built to guard its rulers' secrets. Min Aung Hlaing, whose 2021 coup ousted Suu Kyi, announced her move from a Naypyidaw prison to house arrest in April.

items.title: Anthony Loke says he is ready to face challengers in Chennah as Negeri Sembilan state election looms
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 2 — Incumbent Chennah assemblyman Anthony Loke said he is prepared to face any challenger seeking to contest the seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. Loke, who is also DAP secretary-general, said anyone is free to offer themselves as a candidate in line with the country’s democratic principles, describing electoral contests as a normal feature of democracy. However, Loke reminded anyone intending to contest a state seat to ensure they met the prescribed eligibility requirements, including updating their registered address to the constituency they wished to contest.

items.title: Unemployed man gets 10 years’ jail, two strokes of rotan for raping eight‑year‑old in Cheras flat
items.summary: “For the rape offence, the accused is sentenced to 10 years in prison and one stroke of the cane, while for the physical sexual assault charge, the sentence is four years in prison and one stroke of the cane,” said the judge, who also ordered the prison sentences to run concurrently from the date of the accused’s arrest on September 5, 2025. The court also ordered the man to undergo two years of counselling while in prison and two years of police supervision after completing his sentence. The man is also charged with committing physical sexual assault on the victim at the same place, time and date under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 which carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping.

items.title: Competition Bill 2026 arms MyCC with whistleblower cash rewards, 40pc penalty cuts to strengthen cartel crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) will be empowered to offer cash rewards to whistleblowers and grant greater reductions in financial penalties to enterprises that admit liability early under the Competition (Amendment) Bill 2026, aimed at strengthening action against cartel activities. Armizan said the proposed introduction of new Sections 64A and 64B would also establish a dedicated framework for whistleblower protection and financial rewards, as the existing Whistleblower Protection Act 2010 does not apply to competition cases, which are civil in nature. These amendments will ensure absolute protection for whistleblowers in civil competition cases,” he said.

items.title: Johor’s RM66m wildlife crossing with 1.2km flyover set for 2028, promising to cut roadkill and safeguard lives
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 2 — The RM66 million wildlife crossing project in Jalan Kahang-Mersing is expected to be completed in February 2028 and is believed to be able to reduce wildlife collisions (roadkill), thus protecting property and saving the lives of road users. State Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the 1.2-kilometre crossing with an eight-metre high and 200-metre-long flyover would allow wildlife to cross the road through the space under the flyover. “The Johor State Government is always committed to ensuring a balance between development, public safety and the preservation of the state’s natural and wildlife treasures,” he said in a statement on his Facebook page today.

items.title: High Court throws out bid to overturn Saifuddin defamation ruling, slaps RM8,000 costs on defendant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The High Court in Alor Setar has dismissed a social media user’s application to set aside a default judgment in a defamation suit filed by Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail over posts related to Kedah politics and Kedah Darul Aman FC (KDA FC). In his ruling made available to the media today, Judge Dr John Lee Kien How @ Mohammad Johan Lee dismissed Muhammad Zubair Ismail’s application after finding that the defendant had failed to establish a credible defence to warrant the case proceeding to trial. “As the defendant failed to present a credible defence after disregarding the substituted service mechanism, the court dismisses the application and orders the defendant to pay costs of RM8,000 to the plaintiff (Saifuddin),” he said.

items.title: Belgium eyes tech transfer over rare earths from Malaysia, seeks deeper mineral cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Belgium seeks to strengthen rare earth mineral ties with Malaysia in its bid to reduce reliance on China, the kingdom’s deputy prime minister Maxime Prévot said today. However, Prévot stressed that Belgium wants to strengthen technology transfer with Malaysia in the critical mineral sector rather than merely increasing exports of resources. He said Malaysia could benefit from the expertise of Belgian companies like Solvay and Umicore to efficiently extract different elements within the rare earth supply chain.

items.title: Households in Malaysia waste up to 97kg of food per person each year, stats show
items.summary: He said that based on the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025, food waste is more significant for processed or cooked food compared to raw food, with 94.1 per cent of households reported to have thrown away processed or cooked food compared to 88.7 per cent for raw food. He said that 79.3 per cent of households dispose of food together with other household waste, while only 20.7 per cent practise food waste separation. As for processed or cooked food, he said rice (16.7 per cent) recorded the highest wastage rate, followed by vegetables (15.8 per cent) and food bought from outside (13.8 per cent).

items.title: Hardware store worker in Terengganu accused of raping 16‑year‑old sister, pleads not guilty
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 2 — A 25-year-old man was charged at the Sessions Court here today on two counts of rape and physical sexual assault against his 16-year-old biological sister in January and June last year. The charge was framed under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison sentence of between eight and 30 years and whipping of not less than 10 strokes. The charge was framed under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which provides for imprisonment of up to 20 years and whipping, and an additional prison sentence of up to five years and whipping of not less than two times, if convicted.

items.title: Single mother pleads not guilty to obscene material and altered nude image charges in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA, July 2 — A single mother pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Magistrates’ Court today to possessing obscene material and distributing altered nude images through social media last month. T. V. Pavithra, 33, entered her plea after the two charges were read out by the court interpreter before Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Sulaiman. Deputy public prosecutor Syaza Nur Sharif proposed bail of RM10,000 for each charge, with additional conditions that the accused not disturb the victim, the victim’s family, and prosecution witnesses until the case is concluded.

items.title: Businessman charged with being member of Geng RT6 organised crime group in Sentul since 2020
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — A businessman was charged in the Sessions Court here today with being a member of an organised crime group known as Geng RT6 since 2020. He is accused of being a member of the Geng RT6 organised crime group in Sentul from Jan 14, 2020 to April 27, 2026. They are A. Raja Ratanam, K. Sunthreson, C. Vijaya Kumar, N. Nadarajah, T. Raman, R. Vijayan, M. Kumaaran, N. Harichandran, S. Maran, Suwaran Singh Minder Singh, and Mohammad Atif Khan Mohammad Ali Khan.

items.title: Alleged Kembara driver remanded after police arrest couple over viral Federal Highway crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Police have arrested a husband and wife over a viral crash on the Federal Highway last month, with the man believed to have been behind the wheel of a Perodua Kembara now remanded for four days as investigators move closer to wrapping up the case. Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the pair, both in their 50s, were arrested by the district police’s Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division at about 9pm yesterday. Police had previously appealed for help in tracing the driver of the Perodua Kembara, who allegedly fled after driving dangerously and causing the crash on the Federal Highway heading towards Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Anwar sends congratulations letter to Xi Jinping on China ruling party’s 105th anniversary
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated the Chinese government in conjunction with the 105th anniversary of the Chinese ruling party. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he had sent a letter to Chinese President Xi Jinping, conveying congratulations from the Malaysian government and people. The Prime Minister said he also expressed his appreciation for the long journey of the Chinese ruling party which has shaped the country’s progress based on the strength of its civilization, history and values inherited over a long period of time.

items.title: Mindef clarifies RM571.9m missile payment not advance, made after systems passed RMN tests
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The payment of RM571.9 million to the Norwegian supplier of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) anti-ship missile system for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project was not an advance payment but was made in accordance with the contract’s progress, the Ministry of Defence (Mindef) said. Mindef said that when the payment was made, all NSM systems had been fully manufactured, successfully passed the tests set by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), and were ready for delivery to Malaysia. “Therefore, the payment was made in accordance with the contract terms, and not as an advance payment without physical progress.

items.title: Agriculture Dept says no approval for Malaysian durian exports to China via overland routes
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 2 — The Department of Agriculture (DOA) has never authorised the export of Malaysian fresh durians to China via overland routes, following confirmation from China’s customs authorities that such shipments involving transit countries are not covered under the existing bilateral export protocol. In a statement today, DOA said it had received feedback from the General Administration of Customs of the People’s Republic of China (GACC) that exports of Malaysian fresh durians via overland routes involving transit countries fall outside the scope of the protocol currently agreed between Malaysia and China. DOA said it remained committed to ensuring all agricultural exports complied with the agreed protocols and legal requirements, adding that the current fresh durian export protocol was based solely on export routes mutually approved by Malaysia and China.

items.title: Report claims Jho Low in frame for Trump pardon, White House says he’s ‘not on the radar’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Fugitive Malaysian financier Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, is among those being considered for a possible presidential pardon by US President Donald Trump as part of a proposed Independence Day clemency initiative, The Atlantic reported. Citing three people familiar with the discussions, The Atlantic said Jho Low was among those under consideration. Representatives for Jho Low did not respond to The Atlantic ’s request for comment, the report said.

items.title: Govt to toughen laws on railway trespass and cable theft, harsher penalties coming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The government is finalising amendments to relevant laws to impose stricter action against trespassing, vandalism and theft involving railway assets, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the amendments would, among other things, introduce harsher punishments for cable theft offenders and are expected to be tabled in Parliament later this year. Hasbi said 393 incidents of cable theft were recorded in the past three years involving 19,756 metres of cable with an estimated loss of RM16.3 million.

items.title: Johor teacher accused of bruising seven-year-old pupil with thrown whiteboard eraser claims trial
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 2 — A primary school teacher has been charged with allegedly injuring a seven-year-old pupil after throwing a whiteboard eraser during a lesson, with the child said to have suffered a bruise beneath her right eye. According to New Straits Times , 47-year-old Fakhriah Bustani pleaded not guilty when the charge was read before Magistrate Mustaqim Sukarno at the Batu Pahat Magistrate’s Court today. The eraser allegedly struck the girl below her right eye, leaving a bruise.

items.title: Muar district office denies blocking PM’s June 15 event, says venue change was to keep neutrality
items.summary: MUAR, July 2 — The Muar district office today dismissed allegations that it had obstructed a government event on June 15, clarifying that the programme was instead moved to a different venue for specific administrative reasons. Muar district officer Zainal Eran said the district administration had requested that the event be relocated to uphold the principle of neutrality in the use of government assets, stressing that government buildings should not be used for political programmes or activities. We do not want government buildings to be used for political programmes or political activities,” he told Bernama at the Muar district office here today.

items.title: KL police say fight against bullying and gangsterism must start in primary school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Kuala Lumpur police have stressed that efforts to curb bullying and prevent the influence of gangsterism among students must begin as early as primary school through character-building and discipline-based initiatives. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said although various disciplinary offences had been recorded among primary school pupils, bullying and gangsterism required particular attention before they escalated into more serious problems. Fadil said the police would also not tolerate any form of gangsterism infiltrating educational institutions.

items.title: Not hunger but resale: Why a Sibu magistrate jailed three sisters for stealing 48 cans of corned beef
items.summary: SIBU, July 2 — The Magistrates’ Court here sentenced three sisters from Bintangor to eight months in prison each for stealing 48 cans of corned beef and six packets of seasoning from a mini market. Magistrate Romario Jonoi imposed the sentence on Ngui Mee Ing, 26; Ngui Mee Lang, 25; and Ngui Mee Kah, 22; after accepting their guilty pleas. The police arrested Mee Lang and Mee Kah at the mini market on June 27, before apprehending Mee Ing at a house in Bakong, Meradong on June 29.

items.title: Govt, MCMC move to block VPN loopholes as Malaysia enforces social media age limits
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — The government is working closely with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to combat the use of online tools such as Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) and third-party identities to bypass social media age-limit enforcement. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) will use public information in investigations and address misuse of such technologies, including treating it as an alternative element of an offence. Shamsul Anuar said the main enforcement challenges include rapid technological advancements, data storage limits by telecommunication companies, and platforms and servers located abroad.

items.title: Forget overtime: DOSM reveals Malaysians devote 21.7pc of their day to unpaid household chores
items.summary: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said Malaysians spend 21.7 per cent of their day, or an average of five hours and 12 minutes, on unpaid domestic and care work. Findings from the National Household Indicators Survey (NHIS) 2025 showed that those aged between 45 and 54 devote the most time to unpaid work at five hours and 43 minutes a day, followed by the 25 to 44 age group (five hours and 35 minutes) and those aged 55 to 64 (five hours and 31 minutes). In contrast, individuals aged between 15 and 24 spend the least amount of time on unpaid work, averaging three hours and 53 minutes a day.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng, Tony Pua defend Pakatan’s LRT3 overhaul, say project saved taxpayers RM9.72b
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Former finance minister Lim Guan Eng and former Damansara MP Tony Pua today defended Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) overhaul of the LRT3 project, saying it saved Malaysian taxpayers RM9.72 billion while rejecting suggestions the project had been halted under the coalition’s administration. Lim and Pua said the previous Barisan Nasional administration approved RM9 billion for the project in 2015, but by March 2018, Prasarana had awarded work package contracts worth RM15.2 billion. After accounting for land acquisition, consultancy fees, project delivery partner (PDP) fees and financing costs, they said Prasarana had sought a revised project budget of RM31.65 billion together with an additional RM22 billion government guarantee.

items.title: Anwar rejects ‘inevitable conflict’ narrative in South China Sea, calls for diplomacy over fear
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Malaysia’s Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has rejected the notion that conflict in the South China Sea is inevitable, stressing that dialogue, mutual trust and adherence to international law are essential to preserving regional peace and stability. During a question-and-answer session at the 39th Asia-Pacific Roundtable here on Thursday, Anwar said Malaysia had maintained meaningful engagement with China despite maritime disputes in the South China Sea. “I personally don’t share this phobia about the potential threat to the South China Sea.

items.title: DBKL orders immediate clean-up after viral video reignites scrutiny of Hicks Flats in Bukit Bintang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has ordered immediate clean-up works at Hicks Flats in Bukit Bintang after a viral YouTube video spotlighted the poor condition of the city-centre apartment block. On Monday, DBKL said Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud visited the Jalan Changkat property following complaints and concerns raised by residents and members of the public after the video went viral on social media. “As an immediate measure, the Mayor has directed that cleaning works be carried out without delay to improve conditions in the area,” DBKL said in a statement on Facebook.

items.title: Zahid says PAS’ call to reject Pakatan in Johor polls will only matter if it translates into votes for BN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today welcomed any additional support for Barisan Nasional (BN) after PAS urged voters to reject Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor state election, but said the only measure that mattered would be the outcome at the ballot box on July 11. Speaking to reporters after attending the 2025 Excellent Service Award Ceremony and Kejora Charity Donation at Lotus Desaru in Kota Tinggi, Zahid said BN respected the positions taken by other political coalitions, including PAS’ recent call for voters to withhold support from PH. Asked about claims that the Johor election campaign had been subdued, Zahid dismissed the suggestion, saying BN’s campaign machinery remained active on the ground.
